template_name: Standard Prescription Format – Neonatal Burping Guidance

history: Irritability or excessive crying after feeds

indications_for_burping: Babies with frequent spit-up or gas
Irritable or fussy babies after feeding

non_pharmacological_advice: Burping helps release swallowed air and reduces discomfort
Not mandatory after every feed if baby is calm and comfortable
Always handle baby gently during burping
Use a soft cloth to manage spit-up
Be patient; burping may take 1–2 minutes

when_to_burp.recommendation: After switching sides or at end of feed

burping_techniques.description: Hold baby upright with chin over shoulder; gently pat back
Sit baby upright; support chest and jaw; pat back
Lay baby across lap; support head; gently pat back

practical_tips: Keep baby upright for 10–15 minutes after feeds if spit-up is common
Ensure proper latch or bottle feeding technique to reduce air swallowing
Try different burping positions to see what works best

common_parent_concerns.advice: Acceptable if baby is comfortable and feeding well
Normal; keep baby upright for a few minutes
Gas may have other causes; review feeding technique
Reposition baby and ensure proper support

red_flags: Poor feeding or inadequate weight gain

if_symptoms_persist: Evaluate for GERD or feeding intolerance

parent_counseling: Burping is helpful but not always necessary — डकार दिलाना फ़ायदेमंद है लेकिन हमेशा ज़रूरी नहीं
Do not wake a sleeping, comfortable baby for burping — सोते हुए आराम से लेटे बच्चे को डकार के लिए न जगाएं
Multiple techniques can be tried safely — अलग-अलग तरीके आज़माए जा सकते हैं
Avoid overfeeding and rapid feeding — ज़्यादा दूध या जल्दी-जल्दी दूध न पिलाएं
Seek medical advice if warning signs appear — खतरे के लक्षण दिखें तो डॉक्टर से मिलें

follow_up.interval: Routine well-baby visit or earlier if symptoms

opd_checklist: Baby feeding well and gaining weight?
Any discomfort or excessive crying after feeds?
warning_signs: Projectile or bilious (green) vomiting — उल्टी में हरा रंग या ज़ोर से उल्टी
Persistent abdominal distension — पेट का लगातार फूला रहना
Poor feeding or weight loss — दूध कम पीना या वज़न घटना
Persistent irritability or back arching — लगातार चिड़चिड़ापन या पीठ का तनना
Blood in vomit or stool — उल्टी या मल में खून
expected_course: Most neonates do not require active burping if they are comfortable after feeds. Mild spit-up is normal and resolves by 4-6 months of age. Burping techniques help reduce discomfort from swallowed air, especially in bottle-fed babies.
key_clinical_points: Burping is a supportive measure, not a medical treatment
Not all babies need burping after every feed
Bottle-fed babies swallow more air and benefit more from burping
If baby is comfortable and gaining weight, no intervention needed
Persistent vomiting or poor weight gain warrants further evaluation
